basic_details.job_title: NII Project Research Scientist I Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online
basic_details.short_information: National Institute of Immunology (NII) Project Research Scientist I Notification 2026 has been released on the official website of the National Institute of Immunology. A total of 1 vacancy has been announced through the NII Project Research Scientist I Recruitment 2026.

qualification.education_qualification: First Class Master's degree in any branch of Life Sciences from a recognised university/Institution.
qualification.details: First Class Master's degree in any branch of Life Sciences from a recognised university/Institution. 5 years of research experience in molecular biology including nucleic acid extraction, PCR/RT-PCR, library preparation, Next Generation Sequencing (NGS), genomics workflows, sequencing data quality assessment, and handling of high-throughput sequencing platforms.

how_to_apply: Candidates who wish to apply for NII Project Research Scientist I Recruitment 2026 via email should follow the steps below.
Read the official notification carefully before applying.
Prepare your application with complete bio-data including personal details, educational qualifications, and experience.
Attach self-attested copies of all required documents such as educational certificates, experience certificates, ID proof, and recent passport-size photographs.
Clearly mention the post applied for in the subject line of the email.
Send the complete application to the official email address mentioned in the notification.
Ensure the email is sent before the last date: 10-06-2026.
Keep a copy of the sent email for future reference.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for the NII Project Research Scientist-I (Non-Medical) recruitment?
faq.answer: The last date to submit your application via email is 10th June 2026. Ensure your application reaches before this deadline.

faq.question: Q2. What is the salary offered for the Project Research Scientist-I (Non-Medical) post?
faq.answer: The selected candidate will receive a salary of Rs. 56,000/- per month, along with 27% House Rent Allowance (HRA).

faq.question: Q3. What are the essential qualifications for this position?
faq.answer: A First Class Master's degree in any branch of Life Sciences is required, along with 5 years of specialized research experience in molecular biology and NGS workflows.

faq.question: Q4. How can I apply for this government job opportunity?
faq.answer: Applications are to be submitted via email. You need to send your complete application with all necessary documents to the designated email address before the last date.

faq.question: Q5. Is there any application fee for this recruitment?
faq.answer: Yes, there is an application fee of Rs. 100/- for General/OBC candidates. SC/ST and Women candidates are exempted from paying the fee.

faq.question: Q6. What is the selection process for the Project Research Scientist-I (Non-Medical) role?
faq.answer: The selection will be based on an interview, which may be conducted in offline or online mode.

faq.question: Q7. Does NII offer any age relaxation for reserved categories?
faq.answer: Information regarding age relaxation is not explicitly detailed here. Candidates are advised to check the official notification for specific details.

faq.question: Q8. What is the total number of vacancies announced by NII?
faq.answer: National Institute of Immunology has announced a total of 1 vacancy for the Project Research Scientist-I (Non-Medical) position.

faq.question: Q9. What is the job type for this NII recruitment?
faq.answer: This is a contract-based job opportunity at the National Institute of Immunology.

faq.question: Q10. Where is the National Institute of Immunology located?
faq.answer: The National Institute of Immunology is located in New Delhi, Delhi.
